name: nicotiana rustica
intensity: Strong; significantly more intense than common tobacco
mechanism: Used in shamanic rituals for grounding and spirit communication.
interactions: Other nicotinic agonists and mao inhibitors
May interact dangerously with MAOIs and stimulants
safetyNotes: May cause nausea, tachycardia, vomiting, dizziness at high doses [1 or 0]. Avoid with cardiac issues; Avoid in pregnancy; May interact with MAOIs; Avoid with CNS stimulants; Avoid with cardiovascular disease; Avoid with hypertension; Avoid with psychiatric conditions; Avoid with cardiac issues, may cause traditional supervision often required, dizziness, hypertension, high (dose-dependent nicotine exposure), estimated 30–50 mg nicotine in adults, 0], high risk of nicotine overdose, very toxic, toxic – high nicotine, high nicotine levels can be toxic, overdose possible, especially in children or non-users, hallucinations, bradycardia, moderate to high, ~50 mg total dose can be fatal or may interact with MAOIs; Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, do not combine with serotonergic drugs or MAOIs without medical guidance, nausea, tachycardia, vomiting, dizziness at high doses [1, 0] or may interact with MAOIs.
